selected_important_turn.text: I'd be willing to give you all my firewood if you could give me all the water.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete, high-stakes offer from you and it frames the negotiation as zero-sum by demanding "all the water" in exchange for "all the firewood." It strongly shapes expectations and anchors the division around an all-or-nothing trade.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The offer is overly rigid and extractive—demanding "all" one scarce resource—so it misses obvious integrative possibilities (e.g., trading some water plus other concessions). It risks deadlocking or making the opponent feel exploited, reducing chances of a fair, cooperative outcome.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the demand and open space for trade: ask the opponent for their minimum water need, propose splitting water (e.g., most but not all) or package water+food concessions rather than asking for everything. Phrase trades conditionally and offer alternatives rather than an absolute demand.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (moderate) — creating flexible splits allows more value to be realized for both sides
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (moderate) — reducing the all-or-nothing demand produces a more balanced allocation
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (moderate to large) — less rigid demands make agreement faster and less contentious
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (moderate) — a more cooperative tone preserves trust and goodwill

value2reason.Low: Hi. I need a little fire wood because it rained recently and the woods are wet.
value2reason.Medium: Hi. I need a little more food because it will be winter time when I go camping and thus harder to stay warm.
value2reason.High: Hi. I need a bit more water due to being overweight.

value2reason.Low: I have plenty of food and I can get my own food out of nature.
value2reason.Medium: I need the extra water to prevent the fire from going out of control.
value2reason.High: I get cold and scared at night and I want to always have a fire going.

dialogue.text: Hello. 🙂 What item did you need a little extra of?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Thank you for asking, I would really like some extra fire wood as I usually get cold and scared at night so I can keep a fire going.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'd be willing to give you all my firewood if you could give me all the water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm not sure I can agree to that. I need firewood for warmth and safety. What if I take 2 firewood and you take 1, and we split the water evenly? That way, we both have enough for the night.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your need for firewood, but I value water more. How about I give you 2 water packages and 1 firewood, while you give me 1 water and 2 firewood? That way, we both get what we need without giving everything away.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that’s a fair split. Let’s go with 2 firewood, 1 water for me, and 1 firewood, 2 water for you. It balances our needs and keeps the fire going all night.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I think that’s a fair split. Let’s go with 2 firewood, 1 water for me, and 1 firewood, 2 water for you. It balances our needs and keeps the fire going all night.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Let’s confirm the split: I take 2 firewood and 1 water, and you take 1 firewood and 2 water. That way, the fire stays warm and we both have enough supplies for the night.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Let’s confirm the final split: I take 2 firewood and 1 water, and you take 1 firewood and 2 water. This ensures both of us have enough supplies for the night.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
